clause: This Master Franchise Agreement (“Agreement”) is made and entered into on the 20th day of November, 2018 (the “Effective Date”) at Mumbai, by and between Smaaash Entertainment Private Limited, an Indian company, with its address at Trade View, Level 2, Kamala Mills, Lower Parel, Mumbai 400013, India (“Franchisor”) and I-AM Capital Acquisition Company, a company incorporated in the United States of America, with an address at 1345 Avenue of the Americas, 11th floor, New York, 10105 (“Franchisee”).
risk_explanation: This clause identifies the parties involved, the effective date, and the location of the agreement. It sets the foundational context for the contract.
plain_english: This agreement, called the Master Franchise Agreement, started on November 20, 2018, in Mumbai, between Smaaash Entertainment Private Limited (the Franchisor) from India and I-AM Capital Acquisition Company (the Franchisee) from the USA.

clause: A. WHEREAS, Franchisor operates entertainment centers and gaming arcades throughout India as well as at the Mall of the Americas in Minneapolis Minnesota where Franchisor, through its virtual reality and sports simulation technology and proprietary gamification technologies provides sport and recreational activities/services; and
risk_explanation: This is a 'Whereas' clause, providing background information about the Franchisor's existing business operations and technologies. It establishes the context for the agreement.
plain_english: The Franchisor currently runs entertainment and gaming centers in India and one in Minnesota, using its own virtual reality and sports simulation technology to offer recreational activities.

clause: B. WHEREAS, Franchisee is [•] (add the business of the Franchisee); and
risk_explanation: This clause is incomplete, indicating a critical missing piece of information regarding the Franchisee's business. This omission creates ambiguity and could lead to disputes about the Franchisee's qualifications or scope.
plain_english: This part of the agreement is incomplete and needs to be filled in with a description of what the Franchisee's business is.

clause: C. WHEREAS, Franchisor desires to appoint Franchisee, and Franchisee desires to be so appointed, to act as Franchisor’s exclusive Franchisee in the territories of North America and South America (“Territory”) in the manner set out in this Agreement, for setting up, managing and operating entertainment centres and gaming arcades involving virtual reality, sports simulation technology and proprietary gamification technologies to provide sport and recreational activities/services to the public (“Smaaash Centres”), upon the terms and subject to the conditions hereinafter provided.
risk_explanation: This 'Whereas' clause outlines the mutual intent of the parties: the Franchisor wants to appoint the Franchisee as its exclusive operator in North and South America for Smaaash Centres, and the Franchisee agrees. It defines the scope and territory.
plain_english: The Franchisor wants the Franchisee to be its exclusive partner in North and South America to set up and run Smaaash entertainment centers, and the Franchisee agrees to this role.

clause: 1.1 Rights granted to the Franchisee. Subject to Section 1.2, Franchisor hereby grants to Franchisee the exclusive right, (a) to establish and operate Smaaash Centres in the Territory, (b) to sub-license the right to establish and operate Smaaash Centres to third party franchisees in and for the Territory, (c) a license to use the products and other services developed by Franchisor with respect to the Smaaash Centres (including a right to authorise the use of products and services developed by the Franchisor by third party franchisees), in the Territory, and (d) to identify third party franchisees for the Smaaash Centres in the Territory. The rights granted herein include the limited license to use the Trademarks of the Franchisor (the details of which are morefully set out in Exhibit A), as set out in Section 3 of this Agreement, for the purposes of establishing and operating the Smaaash Centres in the Territory. It is clarified that if third party franchisees shall be establishing and operating Smaaash Centres in the manner contemplated under this Agreement, then the Franchisee shall ensure that such third party franchisees complies with all the obligations and duties of the Franchisee, as recorded under this Agreement, and to this extent, Franchisee may enter into relevant agreements with such third party franchisees.
risk_explanation: This clause clearly defines the extensive exclusive rights granted to the Franchisee, including operating, sub-licensing, using products/services, and identifying sub-franchisees, along with the responsibility to ensure sub-franchisee compliance. It provides a strong foundation for the Franchisee's business model.
plain_english: The Franchisor gives the Franchisee exclusive rights to open and run Smaaash Centers in North and South America, including the ability to let other companies open centers and use the Franchisor's products and trademarks. The Franchisee must make sure any sub-franchisees follow all the rules of this agreement.

clause: 1.2 Notwithstanding the right granted to the Franchisee in Section 1.1, the Franchisor and its affiliates shall retain the right on the terms and conditions that the Franchisor may deem fit and without granting any rights therein to the Franchisee, (i) to own, acquire, establish and / or operate, and to a grant a license to third parties to establish and operate Smaaash Centres at any location outside the Territory, and (ii) to own, acquire, establish and / or operate, and to grant a license to third parties to establish and operate, gaming and entertainment centres under other proprietary marks or other systems, whether such centres are the same, similar or different from the Smaaash Centres, at any location within or outside the Territory.
risk_explanation: This clause clarifies that the Franchisor retains significant rights outside the defined Territory and for other brands, even within the Territory, which is standard for protecting the Franchisor's broader business interests. It limits the exclusivity granted to the Franchisee to the specific Smaaash brand within the Territory.
plain_english: Even though the Franchisee has exclusive rights in its territory, the Franchisor and its related companies can still open Smaaash Centers anywhere else in the world. They can also open other types of gaming and entertainment centers, under different brand names, anywhere in the world, including within the Franchisee's territory.

clause: 1.3 This arrangement has been entered into by the parties on an arms’length basis. All commercials between the parties in relation to the transactions contemplated under this Agreement, if not specifically provided in this Agreement, shall be agreed mutually between the parties.
risk_explanation: The 'arms' length basis' statement is standard, but the provision that unspecified 'commercials' will be mutually agreed upon introduces potential for future disputes or delays if parties cannot reach consensus. It highlights areas requiring further negotiation.
plain_english: The parties are dealing with each other fairly and independently. Any financial details not already written in this agreement will need to be mutually decided by both parties later.

clause: (a) Pricing. Franchisor and Franchisee shall agree upon and decide the locations at which the Smaaash Centres shall be set up within the Territory. The Franchisee shall not be entitled to set up any new Smaaash Centres without consulting with, and obtaining the prior written consent of Smaaash.
risk_explanation: This clause gives the Franchisor significant control over the Franchisee's expansion by requiring mutual agreement on locations and prior written consent for new centers. This could slow down the Franchisee's growth if the Franchisor is not responsive or has different strategic priorities.
plain_english: The Franchisor and Franchisee must agree on where to open new Smaaash Centers. The Franchisee cannot open any new centers without first talking to and getting written permission from the Franchisor.

clause: (b) The prices of the products and services offered in each of the Smaaash Centres shall be decided mutually among the parties. Any revision to the agreed fees, including any discounts or prizes or other promotional measures shall require the prior written consent from Franchisor.
risk_explanation: This clause grants the Franchisor significant control over pricing and promotional activities, requiring mutual agreement for initial pricing and prior written consent for any revisions. This limits the Franchisee's flexibility in responding to local market conditions.
plain_english: The prices for services and products at Smaaash Centers must be agreed upon by both parties. Any changes to these prices, including discounts or promotions, need the Franchisor's written approval first.

clause: (c) Franchisee or the third party franchisee, as the case may be, shall be entitled to receive the revenue generated from each of the Smaaash Centres. If third party franchisees are operating the Smaaash Centres, then the Franchisee shall be entitled to receive, (i) 5% (five percent) of the capital expenditure as agreed among the parties for the particular Smaaash Centre as sign -on fees or upfront advance, and (ii) 5% (five percent) fee or commission of the revenue generated by such third party franchisees from the Smaash Centres on an annual basis.
risk_explanation: This clause clearly outlines the revenue distribution model, specifying that the Franchisee (or sub-franchisee) receives center revenue, and the Franchisee earns a 5% capital expenditure fee and a 5% annual revenue commission from sub-franchisees. This provides clarity on financial terms.
plain_english: The Franchisee or its sub-franchisees will keep the money earned from each Smaaash Center. If sub-franchisees are running the centers, the Franchisee will get a 5% upfront fee based on the center's setup cost and an additional 5% of the sub-franchisee's annual revenue.

clause: 1.5 Operation of Smaaash Centres. Franchisee or third party sub –franchisees shall be under an obligation to set up at least 6 (six) Smaaash Centres during the first Contract Year or any other time period as may be provided by Franchisor. The Smaaash Centres shall be established and operated in the Territory using the assumed trade name ’Smaaash’or any other trade name that the Franchisor may designate. Franchisee shall maintain good customer relations in accordance with prudent and reasonable business practices. Franchisee shall perform its obligations hereunder without using subcontractors, sub-distributors, independent sales representatives, agents, Franchisee’s affiliates or other non-employees (“Third Parties”) to perform the obligations of Franchisee under this Agreement except to the contrary specifically stated in this Agreement or unless they have been approved, in writing, in advance, by Franchisor, such approval not to be unreasonably withheld.
risk_explanation: This clause imposes a strict obligation on the Franchisee to establish at least six centers in the first year, which is a significant performance target. Additionally, it severely restricts the use of third parties for operations without prior written consent, potentially limiting the Franchisee's operational flexibility and increasing direct labor costs.
plain_english: The Franchisee or its sub-franchisees must open at least six Smaaash Centers in the first year, using the 'Smaaash' name or another name chosen by the Franchisor. The Franchisee must also maintain good customer service and cannot use outside contractors or other third parties to fulfill its duties unless the Franchisor gives written permission, which should not be unreasonably denied.

clause: 2. Term The term of this Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date and shall continue until terminated as hereinafter provided (the “Term”). Each calendar year during the Term is sometimes hereinafter referred to as a “Contract Year.”
risk_explanation: This clause clearly defines the commencement of the agreement's term and its continuation until termination, establishing the duration of the contractual relationship. It also defines 'Contract Year.'
plain_english: This agreement starts on the Effective Date and will continue until it is ended as described later. Each full year of the agreement is called a 'Contract Year.'

clause: 3.1 License. Subject to the terms of this Agreement (including all obligations to first obtain Franchisor’s written approval), Franchisor hereby grants to Franchisee the right to use the Trademarks (the details of which are set out in Exhibit A to this Agreement) (including sub-licensing this right to third party franchisees with the approval of Franchisor), on a royalty-free basis, for the purpose of operating and promoting the Smaaash Centres in the Territory. Franchisee is, in particular entitled to:
risk_explanation: This clause grants the Franchisee a royalty-free license to use the Franchisor's trademarks for operating and promoting Smaaash Centres, including sub-licensing with Franchisor approval. This is a key benefit for the Franchisee, though subject to Franchisor's written approval.
plain_english: The Franchisor gives the Franchisee a free license to use its trademarks to run and advertise Smaaash Centers in the territory, and the Franchisee can also let sub-franchisees use them with the Franchisor's permission.

clause: (a) offer, market and/or distribute any products and services in connection with the Smaaash Centres under the Trademarks; and
risk_explanation: This clause specifies one of the rights granted under the trademark license: to offer, market, and distribute products and services related to Smaaash Centres using the Trademarks. This is a standard and expected right for a franchisee.
plain_english: The Franchisee is allowed to sell, market, and distribute products and services related to Smaaash Centers using the Franchisor's trademarks.

clause: b) use the Trademarks on business stationery and/or in advertising in connection with the advertising, promotion and distribution of Smaaash Centres in the Territory.
risk_explanation: This clause specifies another right granted under the trademark license: to use the Trademarks on business stationery and in advertising materials for promoting Smaaash Centres. This is a standard and necessary right for a franchisee.
plain_english: The Franchisee can use the trademarks on its business documents and in advertisements to promote Smaaash Centers in its territory.

clause: 3.2 Exclusivity of License. Except as provided in the next sentence, the license granted herein shall be exclusive. “Exclusivity” shall mean that Franchisor shall not grant any further licenses to third parties in the Trademarks for use in connection with Smaaash Centres in the Territory, and the Franchisee shall not enter into any arrangement or agreement with any third parties for establishing or operating any gaming and entertainment centres identical or similar to Smaaash Centres, in the Territory except as otherwise provided in this Agreement; provided, however, that Franchisor may continue to use the Trademarks in the Territory in connection with the operation of Franchisor’s entertainment centers already set up as of the Effective Date in the Territory. The restriction contained in this Agreement shall apply on the parties throughout the Term.
risk_explanation: While granting exclusivity to the Franchisee for Smaaash Centres, this clause also imposes a significant restriction on the Franchisee, preventing them from operating any similar gaming centers in the Territory. Furthermore, the Franchisor retains the right to operate its existing Smaaash centers in the Territory, which slightly dilutes the Franchisee's exclusivity.
plain_english: The Franchisee has exclusive rights to use the trademarks for Smaaash Centers in its territory, meaning the Franchisor won't license them to others there. However, the Franchisor can still run any Smaaash Centers it already had in the territory when the agreement started. The Franchisee is also restricted from opening any similar entertainment centers in the territory.

clause: 3.3 Form of Use. Unless otherwise provided herein or agreed by the parties in writing, Franchisee shall use the Trademarks that are registered in the Territory in their registered form.
risk_explanation: This clause requires the Franchisee to use registered trademarks in their registered form, which is a standard practice to maintain brand consistency and protect intellectual property. It allows for flexibility if agreed upon in writing.
plain_english: The Franchisee must use the trademarks exactly as they are registered in the territory, unless the agreement says otherwise or both parties agree to a different use in writing.

clause: 4. Compliance with Law Franchisee shall ensure that the Smaaash Centres shall be set up, established, operated, managed, advertised, marketed, promoted, publicized and otherwise exploited, in accordance with all applicable laws and regulations in the Territory, including without limitation, all customs requirements and country of origin regulations.
risk_explanation: This clause places a standard and necessary obligation on the Franchisee to comply with all applicable laws and regulations in the Territory for all aspects of the Smaaash Centres' operations. This is a fundamental requirement for any business.
plain_english: The Franchisee must make sure that all Smaaash Centers are set up, run, and promoted according to all local laws and regulations in the territory, including rules about customs and product origin.

clause: (a) Franchisee shall exercise its best efforts to effectively market, promote, and publicise the Smaaash Centres throughout the Territory. Franchisee shall also be obligated to identify suitable locations to set up the Smaaash Centres. Franchisee shall comply with, and ensure that the third party sub-franchisees comply with the standards prescribed by Franchisor (as provided in the operating manuals which shall be shared by Franchisor with Franchisee) with respect to the services, products and operations of the Smaaash Centres and shall operate the Smaaash Centres in strict conformity with such standards and specifications as Franchisor may from time to time prescribe to Franchisee. Franchisee shall refrain from deviating from such standards and specifications without Franchisor’s prior written consent and from otherwise operating in any manner which reflects adversely on the Trademarks and Smaaash Centres.
risk_explanation: This clause imposes a 'best efforts' obligation for marketing and promotion, which can be subjective, and requires strict adherence to Franchisor's standards and operating manuals. This gives the Franchisor significant control over operations and brand image, potentially limiting the Franchisee's autonomy.
plain_english: The Franchisee must do its best to market and promote Smaaash Centers and find good locations for them. The Franchisee, and any sub-franchisees, must strictly follow all operating standards and specifications set by the Franchisor, and cannot change them without written permission, to protect the brand's reputation.

clause: (b) Unless otherwise agreed by Franchisor in writing, throughout the Term, Franchisee shall maintain, and shall ensure that the third party sub –franchisees maintain an organizational structure or local management reasonably necessary to adequately support the advertising, marketing and promotion of the Smaaash Centres and the services and products offered by Smaaash Centres throughout the Territory. The third party sub –franchisees shall also be responsible for all employee related compliances as per the relevant applicable laws. Towards this purpose, Franchisee shall appoint a qualified chief operating officer, in consultation with Franchisor, to undertake and manage the obligations of Franchisee as set out in this Agreement. Franchisor shall also be entitled to designate and appoint personnel from its managerial team to assist and train the personnel and staff of the Franchisee or any other third party sub - franchisee in setting up the Smaaash Centres, and further provide technical and design knowledge to the third party sub - franchisees.
risk_explanation: This clause places a significant operational burden on the Franchisee, requiring a specific organizational structure, a qualified COO appointed in consultation with the Franchisor, and responsibility for sub-franchisee employee compliance. While the Franchisor offers assistance, the requirements for management structure and consultation could limit the Franchisee's independent decision-making.
plain_english: The Franchisee must maintain a suitable management structure and ensure sub-franchisees do the same to support Smaaash Centers' marketing. Sub-franchisees are responsible for employee compliance. The Franchisee must hire a qualified chief operating officer, with the Franchisor's input, to manage its duties. The Franchisor can also send its staff to help train the Franchisee's and sub-franchisees' employees and provide technical knowledge.

clause: 5.2 Promotional Material and Products. Franchisee shall submit to Franchisor, for Franchisor’s prior written approval, samples of all advertising and promotional materials that Franchisee desires to use to promote Smaaash Centres, including without limitation, print and online advertising designs, trade show display materials, press releases and interviews for publication in any media (“Promotional Material”). Franchisee shall modify any disapproved Promotional Material to satisfy Franchisor’s reasonable objections so that it is acceptable to Franchisor. Franchisor shall provide Franchisee with the creative elements of any Promotional Materials that Franchisor creates or acquires for use in connection with the advertising and sale of Products outside the Territory.
risk_explanation: This clause gives the Franchisor complete control over all promotional materials, requiring prior written approval and the right to demand modifications. This ensures brand consistency but significantly limits the Franchisee's creative and marketing autonomy, potentially delaying campaigns.
plain_english: The Franchisee must get written approval from the Franchisor for all advertising and promotional materials before using them. If the Franchisor disapproves, the Franchisee must make changes until they are acceptable. The Franchisor will also share its own promotional materials for use outside the territory.

clause: (a) Franchisor is the sole owner of any and all intellectual property rights relating to the Smaaash Centres and their products and services existing as of the Effective Date, including, but not limited to, the Trademarks and all the goodwill relating thereto (the “Franchisor Property”). Franchisee, or any third party franchisee, by reason of this Agreement, has not and shall not acquire any right, title, interest or claim of ownership in any of the Franchisor Property in the Territory or elsewhere, except to the extent provided under the license granted under Sections 1 and 3 of this Agreement.
risk_explanation: This clause clearly establishes the Franchisor's sole ownership of all intellectual property related to Smaaash Centres and explicitly states that the Franchisee acquires no ownership rights, only a limited license. This is standard for franchise agreements to protect the franchisor's core assets.
plain_english: The Franchisor owns all intellectual property related to Smaaash Centers, including trademarks and brand reputation. The Franchisee and any sub-franchisees do not gain ownership of this property, only a limited right to use it as specified in the agreement.

clause: (b) Franchisee acknowledges that, (i) Franchisor is the sole and exclusive owner of all right, title and interest in any Franchisor Property; (ii) nothing contained in this Agreement shall give to Franchisee any right, title or interest in any Franchisor Property; and (iii) Franchisee’s use of the Franchisor Property, and any associated goodwill, shall inure only to the benefit of Franchisor and shall be deemed to be solely the property of Franchisor should this Agreement be terminated for any reason.
risk_explanation: This clause reinforces the Franchisor's exclusive ownership of all intellectual property and clarifies that any goodwill generated by the Franchisee's use benefits only the Franchisor. This is a standard and crucial protection for the franchisor's brand assets.
plain_english: The Franchisee agrees that the Franchisor is the only owner of all its intellectual property, and this agreement does not give the Franchisee any ownership rights. Any positive reputation gained from the Franchisee's use of this property will belong solely to the Franchisor, especially if the agreement ends.

clause: 6.2 Registration and Cooperation. Franchisee shall not, directly or indirectly, seek or obtain any new registration for Franchisor Property (including without limitation, any colorable imitations, translations, or transliterations thereof), anywhere in the world without Franchisor’s prior written consent. If Franchisee has obtained or obtains in the future, in any country, any right, title or interest in any Franchisor Property notwithstanding the previous sentence (including any colorable imitations, translations, or transliterations thereof), Franchisee will be deemed to have so acted as an agent and for the benefit of Franchisor for the limited purpose of obtaining such registrations and assigning them to Franchisor. Franchisee shall execute, for no additional consideration, any and all documents deemed necessary by Franchisor or its attorneys to be necessary to transfer such right, title or interest to Franchisor.
risk_explanation: This clause strictly prohibits the Franchisee from registering any of the Franchisor's intellectual property and mandates that any such registrations, if obtained, are for the Franchisor's benefit and must be transferred without additional cost. This is a critical protection for the Franchisor's global IP rights.
plain_english: The Franchisee cannot try to register any of the Franchisor's intellectual property anywhere in the world without the Franchisor's written permission. If the Franchisee does obtain any such registrations, they will be considered to have done so on behalf of the Franchisor and must transfer them to the Franchisor without being paid extra.

clause: 6.3 No Challenges. Franchisee shall not do anything or suffer anything to be done which may adversely affect any rights of Franchisor in and to any Franchisor Property, or any registrations thereof or which, directly or indirectly, may reduce or dilute the value or distinctiveness of such Franchisor Property, in particular the Trademarks, or disparage or detract from Franchisor’s reputation. Franchisee shall not challenge, directly or indirectly, Franchisor’s interest in, or the validity of, any Franchisor Property, or any application for registration or trademark registration thereof or any rights of Franchisor therein. The provisions of this Section 6.3 shall survive the termination of this Agreement.
risk_explanation: This clause imposes a broad and perpetual obligation on the Franchisee not to challenge, dilute, or disparage the Franchisor's intellectual property or reputation. This is a standard and essential protection for the franchisor's brand and IP assets, surviving termination.
plain_english: The Franchisee must not do anything that could harm the Franchisor's intellectual property rights, reduce the value of its trademarks, or damage its reputation. The Franchisee also cannot question the Franchisor's ownership or the validity of its intellectual property. These rules continue even after the agreement ends.

clause: (a) Mutual Information. Each of the parties shall inform the other without undue delay when such party becomes aware of any infringements of any of the Franchisor Property in the Territory.
risk_explanation: This clause establishes a mutual obligation for both parties to promptly inform each other of any intellectual property infringements they become aware of. This ensures timely action can be taken to protect the Franchisor's assets.
plain_english: Both parties must quickly tell the other if they find out about anyone illegally using the Franchisor's intellectual property in the territory.

clause: (b) Initiation of Action. Any actions against infringers of any of the Franchisor Property, whether or not such actions involve litigation (including any actions taken to oppose a third party application to register an infringing trademark or a cancellation action against a third party’s infringing trademark registration), shall be exclusively reserved to Franchisor, unless otherwise agreed by Franchisor in writing. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Franchisor shall be under no obligation to initiate any such action. If requested by Franchisor, Franchisee shall support Franchisor, at Franchisor’s expense, in any such proceedings and, if requested by Franchisor, Franchisee shall promptly provide Franchisor with any relevant documentation in Franchisee’s possession.
risk_explanation: This clause grants the Franchisor exclusive control over initiating legal actions against infringers, but also states the Franchisor is under no obligation to do so. This could leave the Franchisee vulnerable if the Franchisor chooses not to defend its IP, though the Franchisee is required to assist at the Franchisor's expense.
plain_english: Only the Franchisor can decide to take action against those who infringe on its intellectual property, unless agreed otherwise in writing, and the Franchisor is not required to take action. If the Franchisor does act, the Franchisee must help, providing documents and support, with the Franchisor covering the costs.

clause: 7.2 Attacks on the Use of the Franchisor Property. Each of the parties shall inform the other if it becomes aware of a claim by a third party that the use of any of any of the Franchisor Property infringes on the rights of such third party. If requested by Franchisor, Franchisee shall support Franchisor, at Franchisor’s expense, in connection with Franchisor’s defense against any such third party claims. Unless otherwise agreed by Franchisor in writing, Franchisor shall take the lead in any defense against a third party action, whether brought against Franchisor and/or Franchisee. The decision whether or not a defense is appropriate shall be in Franchisor’s sole discretion. Franchisee shall not settle any third party claims against it regarding its use of any of the Franchisor Property without the prior written consent of Franchisor.
risk_explanation: This clause requires mutual notification of third-party infringement claims and mandates Franchisee support at Franchisor's expense. However, it gives the Franchisor sole discretion over defense strategy and prohibits the Franchisee from settling claims without consent, potentially exposing the Franchisee to liability if the Franchisor decides not to defend.
plain_english: Both parties must inform each other if a third party claims the Franchisor's intellectual property infringes on their rights. The Franchisee must help the Franchisor defend against such claims, with the Franchisor paying the costs. The Franchisor will lead the defense and decide whether to defend, and the Franchisee cannot settle any claims without the Franchisor's written approval.

clause: 7.3 Indemnity. The Franchisee shall indemnify and hold the Franchisor, its affiliates and their respective agents and employees harmless from all claims, actions, suits, damages, costs and expenses in relation to or arising out of the breach of any representations, warranties, covenants and obligations of the Franchisee as set out in this Agreement. The indemnification rights of the Franchisor shall be without prejudice to, and independent of any other rights and remedies that the Franchisor may have at law or in equity, including the right to seek specific performance, injunctive relief or restitution, none of which rights or remedies shall be affected or diminished thereby. The provisions of this Section 7.3 shall survive the termination of this Agreement.
risk_explanation: This is a broad indemnity clause placing significant financial risk on the Franchisee, requiring them to cover all costs and damages arising from their breaches. It also explicitly states that this indemnity is in addition to all other legal and equitable remedies available to the Franchisor, and it survives termination.
plain_english: The Franchisee must protect the Franchisor and its related parties from all claims, costs, and damages that result from the Franchisee breaking any part of this agreement. This protection is in addition to any other legal actions the Franchisor can take, and it remains in effect even after the agreement ends.

clause: 8.1 Termination by Mutual Agreement. This Agreement may be terminated at any time upon the mutual written agreement of the parties.
risk_explanation: This clause provides a straightforward and amicable way for both parties to terminate the agreement by mutual written consent. This is a standard and fair provision.
plain_english: Both parties can end this agreement at any time if they both agree to it in writing.

clause: 8.2 Termination by Franchisor with Notice. Franchisor may terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days written notice to Franchisee upon the occurrence of any of the following:
risk_explanation: This clause introduces specific conditions under which the Franchisor can terminate the agreement with 30 days' notice. While it favors the Franchisor, it provides clarity on the grounds for termination.
plain_english: The Franchisor can end this agreement by giving the Franchisee 30 days' written notice if certain events listed below happen.

clause: (a) Franchisee fails to make any payment required under or in connection with this Agreement;
risk_explanation: This is a standard termination clause, allowing the Franchisor to terminate if the Franchisee fails to make required payments. This protects the Franchisor's financial interests.
plain_english: The Franchisor can end the agreement if the Franchisee doesn't make any payments required by the agreement.

clause: (b) Franchisee ceases to operate or otherwise abandon the Smaaash Centres without the consent of Franchisor, or otherwise forfeit the right to do or transact business in the Territory;
risk_explanation: This is a standard termination clause, allowing the Franchisor to terminate if the Franchisee ceases operations or abandons the centers without consent, or loses the right to do business. This protects the brand's presence and the Franchisor's investment.
plain_english: The Franchisor can end the agreement if the Franchisee stops operating or abandons the Smaaash Centers without permission, or loses its right to do business in the territory.

clause: (c) Franchisee fails to use its best efforts to market and promote Smaaash Centres and the services and products offered by Smaaash Centres within the Territory and such failure is not cured within thirty (30) days of Franchisor’s notification to Franchisee of such failure.
risk_explanation: This clause allows termination if the Franchisee fails to use 'best efforts' in marketing and promotion, which is a subjective standard. While a 30-day cure period is provided, the subjective nature of 'best efforts' could lead to disputes and places a continuous burden on the Franchisee.
plain_english: The Franchisor can end the agreement if the Franchisee doesn't try its best to market and promote Smaaash Centers, and doesn't fix this issue within 30 days of being told by the Franchisor.

clause: 8.3 Termination for Cause. This Agreement may be terminated by either party for “Cause” without the need of providing a notice period prior to such termination becoming effective. “Cause” shall exist if circumstances occur which, taking into consideration the substance and purpose of this Agreement, would make it unreasonable for one or both of the parties to continue the contractual relationship and the other party fails to cure the cause (assuming that such cause is susceptible to cure) within thirty (30) days after the date of receipt of a corresponding written notice (“Remedy Notice”). If such cause by its nature is not curable, then no such Remedy Notice is required. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, a party may terminate this Agreement for “Cause”if:
risk_explanation: This clause defines 'Termination for Cause,' allowing either party to end the agreement immediately for serious breaches that make continuation unreasonable, with a 30-day cure period for curable issues. This provides a clear framework for addressing fundamental problems.
plain_english: Either party can immediately end this agreement for 'Cause' if something serious happens that makes it unreasonable to continue the contract, and the other party fails to fix the problem within 30 days of being notified (if it can be fixed). If the problem cannot be fixed, no notice is needed.

clause: (a) the other party to this Agreement is in breach of one or more of its material obligations; or
risk_explanation: This is a standard 'for cause' termination ground, allowing termination for a material breach of obligations. This is a fundamental protection for both parties in a contract.
plain_english: One reason for 'Cause' termination is if the other party breaks one or more of its important duties in the agreement.

clause: (b) the other party to this Agreement becomes insolvent, generally cannot pay its obligations when due or otherwise suffers a substantial deterioration of its financial situation, or if insolvency/bankruptcy proceedings are initiated against such party or such party initiates any dissolution or liquidation of its business and/or assets.
risk_explanation: This is a standard 'for cause' termination ground, allowing termination if a party experiences financial distress, insolvency, or bankruptcy. This protects the other party from continuing a relationship with a financially unstable partner.
plain_english: Another reason for 'Cause' termination is if the other party becomes unable to pay its debts, faces bankruptcy, or starts to close down its business.

clause: (a) Upon the termination of this Agreement, any indebtedness of Franchisee to Franchisor shall become immediately due and payable. Franchisee shall immediately cease to operate the Smaaash Centres and shall not thereafter, directly or indirectly, represent to the public or hold itself out as a franchisee of Franchisor. Franchisor shall have the right to suspend the performance of any of their obligations under this Agreement. Franchisor shall have the right to provide the rights and license granted herein to Franchisee to any other third party entity that Franchisor may deem fit.
risk_explanation: This clause outlines significant consequences for the Franchisee upon termination, including immediate payment of debts, cessation of operations, and loss of representation. It also grants the Franchisor the right to suspend its obligations and re-license the territory, which is standard but impactful for the Franchisee.
plain_english: When the agreement ends, any money the Franchisee owes the Franchisor must be paid right away. The Franchisee must immediately stop running Smaaash Centers and cannot claim to be a Franchisor franchisee anymore. The Franchisor can stop fulfilling its duties and can give the Franchisee's former rights and license to another company.

clause: (b) All benefits which may accrue by reason of the activities of Franchisee hereunder shall be deemed transferred automatically to Franchisor, and all licenses and other rights granted to Franchisee hereunder shall immediately cease. Unless otherwise agreed by Franchisor in writing, Franchisee shall immediately discontinue the advertising and marketing of Smaaash Centres and the products and services offered by Smaaash Centres.
risk_explanation: This clause clearly states that upon termination, all benefits generated by the Franchisee's activities transfer to the Franchisor, and all Franchisee rights and licenses immediately cease. This is a standard and necessary provision to protect the Franchisor's brand and business continuity.
plain_english: When the agreement ends, any benefits the Franchisee gained will automatically go to the Franchisor, and all rights and licenses given to the Franchisee will stop immediately. The Franchisee must also stop all advertising and marketing of Smaaash Centers unless the Franchisor agrees otherwise in writing.

clause: (c) Each of the parties shall continue to maintain in confidence any and all confidential information received from the other party. At Franchisor’s election, Franchisor may purchase from Franchisee any materials used by Franchisee for the advertising, marketing, promotion, publicizing or other exploitation of Smaaash Centres and the products and services offered by the Smaaash Centres, including all Promotional Materials, Franchisor Property, or any other materials which contain any of the Trademarks.
risk_explanation: This clause includes a standard confidentiality obligation that survives termination and grants the Franchisor an option to purchase marketing materials from the Franchisee. This protects confidential information and allows the Franchisor to potentially recover useful assets.
plain_english: Both parties must keep all confidential information secret, even after the agreement ends. The Franchisor has the option to buy any advertising or promotional materials from the Franchisee that were used for Smaaash Centers, including those with the Franchisor's trademarks.

clause: (d) The termination of this Agreement for any reason shall not affect obligations accrued prior to the effective date of such termination of this Agreement or any obligations which, either expressly or from the context of this Agreement, are intended to survive the termination of this Agreement.
risk_explanation: This is a standard 'survival' clause, ensuring that obligations incurred before termination and those explicitly stated to survive termination remain enforceable. This provides legal clarity and continuity for certain contractual duties.
plain_english: Ending this agreement, for any reason, does not cancel any duties or debts that were already in place before the termination date, or any duties that are meant to continue after the agreement ends.

clause: 9. Notices and Other Communications All reports, approvals, requests, demands, notices and other communications (collectively “Communications”) required or permitted by this Agreement shall be in writing and signed by a duly authorized officer of or such other individual designated in writing by a party. Communications will be duly given if delivered personally, if mailed (by registered mail, return receipt requested) or if delivered by nationally-recognized courier or mail service which requires the addressee to acknowledge, in writing, the receipt thereof, to the party concerned at the following addresses (or at any other address as a party may specify by notice in writing to the other): If to Franchisor: Smaaash Entertainment Private Limited Trade View, Level 2 Kamala Mills Lower Parel, Mumbai 400013, India Attention: Mr. Vishwanath Kotian If to Franchisee: 1345 Avenue of the Americas, 11th floor New York, NY 101015, USA Attention: Mr. Suhel Kanuga
risk_explanation: This clause clearly defines the requirements for all official communications, including format, authorized signatories, and delivery methods and addresses. This is a standard and important administrative provision to ensure proper notice.
plain_english: All official communications related to this agreement must be in writing, signed by an authorized person, and sent to the specified addresses by personal delivery, registered mail, or a courier service that requires a written receipt.

clause: 10.1 Entire Agreement. This Agreement contains the entire understanding and agreement between the parties with respect to its subject matter, supersedes all prior oral or written understandings and agreements relating thereto and may not be modified, discharged or terminated, nor may any of the provisions hereof be waived, orally.
risk_explanation: This is a standard 'entire agreement' clause, stating that the written agreement is the complete and final understanding between the parties, superseding all prior discussions, and cannot be modified orally. This prevents reliance on unwritten promises.
plain_english: This document is the complete and final agreement between the parties, replacing all previous discussions or agreements, whether spoken or written. It cannot be changed or ended by spoken agreement.

clause: 10.2 Right to inspect and request information. During the Term, Franchisor shall have the right to conduct audits of Franchisee with respect to the Smaaash Centres, and inspect the Smaaash Centres, after providing a written notice of 5 (five) days. Franchisee shall be under an obligation to provide any information as may be requested by Franchisor with respect to the Smaaash Centres, including the books of accounts and other relevant documents or records maintained in relation to the Smaaash Centres.
risk_explanation: This clause grants the Franchisor broad audit and inspection rights with only five days' notice, and obligates the Franchisee to provide any requested information, including financial records. This ensures Franchisor oversight but can be intrusive for the Franchisee.
plain_english: The Franchisor can audit and inspect the Franchisee's Smaaash Centers and request any related information, including financial records, with five days' written notice.

clause: 10.3 Insurance. During the Term, Franchisee shall maintain policies of insurance as may be requested by Franchisor, subject to applicable law, in relation to the Smaaash Centres.
risk_explanation: This clause obligates the Franchisee to maintain insurance policies as requested by the Franchisor, which could lead to unexpected costs or requirements if the Franchisor demands extensive coverage. While standard, the open-ended nature of 'as may be requested' places the burden on the Franchisee.
plain_english: The Franchisee must keep insurance policies for the Smaaash Centers as requested by the Franchisor, as long as it complies with the law.

clause: 10.4 Representations and warranties. Each of the parties represents and warrants to the other party that, (i) the Agreement constitutes a valid, legal and binding obligation of such party and is enforceable against such party in accordance with its terms, (ii) it has the power and authority to execute the Agreement and perform all its terms, and (iii) the execution and performance of this Agreement shall not violate any charter documents of such party, contravene any provisions of law as applicable to such party (including any order, decree, injunction of any competent court) or conflict with the provisions of any material agreement or contract executed by such party. The provisions of this Section 10.4 shall survive the termination of this Agreement.
risk_explanation: This clause contains standard mutual representations and warranties regarding the parties' legal capacity, authority, and non-violation of other agreements or laws. This ensures both parties are legally able to enter and perform the contract, and these provisions survive termination.
plain_english: Both parties promise that this agreement is legally binding, they have the authority to sign and fulfill it, and doing so won't break any of their company rules, laws, or other important contracts. These promises remain true even after the agreement ends.

clause: 10.5 Governing Law. (a) The parties hereto have expressly agreed that this Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the laws of the State of New York, applicable to contracts executed and fully to be performed therein, to the exclusion of any other applicable body of governing law.
risk_explanation: This clause clearly designates New York State law as the governing law for the agreement, providing certainty and predictability in legal interpretation. This is a standard and important choice of law provision.
plain_english: Both parties agree that this agreement will be interpreted and governed by the laws of New York State, and no other laws will apply.

clause: (b) Except as hereafter provided, the parties hereby consent to the jurisdiction of the New York State Supreme Court, County of New York or in the United States District Court for the Southern District of New York to resolve any dispute arising under this Agreement.
risk_explanation: This clause establishes the exclusive jurisdiction for dispute resolution in New York State courts, providing a clear forum for any legal actions. This is a standard and important choice of forum provision.
plain_english: Unless stated otherwise later, both parties agree that any disagreements about this agreement will be handled by the New York State Supreme Court in New York County or the U.S. District Court for the Southern District of New York.

clause: (c) In the event of any litigation or other action arising out of this Agreement, the court shall award to the substantially prevailing party all reasonable costs and expenses including reasonable attorney’s fees.
risk_explanation: This clause provides for the recovery of legal costs and attorney's fees by the substantially prevailing party in any dispute. This is a standard provision that can incentivize settlement and deter frivolous litigation.
plain_english: If there's a lawsuit or other legal action related to this agreement, the party who mostly wins the case will be awarded all reasonable costs and attorney's fees.

clause: 10.6 WAIVER OF JURY. THE PARTIES HEREBY WAIVE ANY RIGHT TO TRIAL BY JURY IN ANY PROCEEDING ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT OR ANY OF THE TRANSACTIONS CONTEMPLATED UNDER THIS AGREEMENT, WHETHER NOW OR EXISTING OR HEREAFTER ARISING, AND WHETHER SOUNDING IN CONTRACT, TORT OR OTHERWISE. THE PARTIES AGREE THAT ANY OF THEM MAY FILE A COPY OF THIS SECTION WITH ANY COURT AS WRITTEN EVIDENCE OF THE KNOWING, VOLUNTARY AND BARGAINED FOR AGREEMENT AMONG THE PARTIES IRREVOCABLY TO WAIVE THE TRIAL BY JURY COURT, AND THAT ANY PROCEEDINGS WHATSOEVER BETWEEN THEM RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT SHALL INSTEAD BE TRIED IN A COURT OF COMPETENT JURISDICTION BY A JUDGE SITTING WITHOUT A JURY.
risk_explanation: This clause is a significant waiver of the right to a jury trial, meaning any disputes will be decided by a judge. While common in commercial contracts, it removes a fundamental legal right and can alter the dynamics of litigation.
plain_english: Both parties give up their right to have a jury decide any legal disputes related to this agreement. Instead, all such cases will be heard and decided by a judge without a jury.

clause: 10.7 Force Majeure. The parties will not be liable to each other for any failure or delay in performance, other than failure to make timely payments due under this Agreement, if it is because of earthquake, flood, fire, acts of God, civil unrest, terrorism, acts of any governmental authority or any other reason beyond the reasonable control of either or both of the parties (“Force Majeure”). However, either party may terminate this Agreement by and upon notice to the other if the other is unable to perform any of its material obligations for a period of thirty (30) days by reason of a Force Majeure.
risk_explanation: This clause provides standard force majeure protection, excusing non-performance (except for payments) due to uncontrollable events. It also includes a termination right if a material obligation is affected for 30 days, offering a clear exit strategy in prolonged force majeure situations.
plain_english: Neither party will be held responsible for delays or failures (except for payments) caused by events beyond their control, like natural disasters or government actions. However, if one party cannot perform its main duties for 30 days due to such an event, the other party can end the agreement.

clause: 10.8 No Joint Venture. Nothing herein is intended to constitute the parties as partners or as joint venturers, or either as agent of the other, and neither party may obligate or bind the other.
risk_explanation: This is a standard 'no partnership/joint venture' clause, explicitly stating that the parties are independent contractors and not agents of each other. This is important for limiting liability and maintaining distinct legal identities.
plain_english: This agreement does not create a partnership or joint venture between the parties, nor does it make one party an agent of the other. Neither party can legally commit the other.

clause: 10.9 Headings, Definitions and other particulars. Headings and titles of sections and/or paragraphs are for convenience only. The definitions in this Agreement shall apply equally to both the singular and plural forms of the terms defined. Whenever the context may require, any pronoun shall include the corresponding masculine, feminine and neuter forms. The use of “including” in this Agreement shall be construed as illustrative.
risk_explanation: This is a standard boilerplate clause clarifying how headings, definitions, and certain linguistic conventions (singular/plural, pronouns, 'including') should be interpreted within the agreement. It aids in consistent understanding.
plain_english: The headings in this agreement are just for convenience. Definitions apply to both singular and plural forms, pronouns cover all genders, and the word 'including' means 'for example,' not 'only.'

clause: 10.10 Amendment. This Agreement shall, from the Effective Date, bind the parties to the terms herein and cannot be amended without the consent of the parties. Further, this Agreement cannot be terminated by any party except in accordance with Clause 8 of this Agreement.
risk_explanation: This clause clearly states that the agreement is binding from the effective date, requires mutual consent for amendments, and restricts termination solely to the methods outlined in Clause 8. This provides stability and clarity regarding changes and termination.
plain_english: This agreement is binding from its start date and can only be changed if both parties agree. Also, neither party can end this agreement unless it's done exactly as described in Clause 8.

clause: 10.11 Assignment. The Franchisor shall be entitled to assign, transfer, encumber or dispose of any of its rights and or obligations under this Agreement, including to an affiliate, without the prior written consent of the Franchisee. The Franchisee shall not be entitled to assign, transfer, encumber or dispose of any of its rights and or obligations under this Agreement, including to an affiliate, without the prior written consent of the Franchisor.
risk_explanation: This clause creates an imbalance in assignment rights: the Franchisor can assign its rights and obligations without Franchisee consent, but the Franchisee cannot do so without Franchisor's prior written consent. This gives the Franchisor significant flexibility while restricting the Franchisee.
plain_english: The Franchisor can transfer its rights and duties under this agreement to another party, even a related company, without asking the Franchisee for permission. However, the Franchisee cannot transfer its rights or duties without first getting written approval from the Franchisor.

clause: 10.12 Expenses. The Franchisee shall bear all the costs and expenses in relation to the execution of this Agreement and the consummation of all the transactions hereunder.
risk_explanation: This clause places the entire burden of all costs and expenses related to the agreement's execution and transactions solely on the Franchisee. This can represent a significant and potentially open-ended financial obligation for the Franchisee.
plain_english: The Franchisee is responsible for paying all costs and expenses related to signing this agreement and completing all the business deals mentioned in it.

clause: 10.13 Counterparts. This Agreement may be executed in two (2) or more counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, but all of which shall constitute one and the same instrument. The delivery of signed counterparts by facsimile transmission or electronic mail in “portable document format” (“.pdf”) shall be as effective as signing and delivering the document in person.
risk_explanation: This is a standard boilerplate clause allowing the agreement to be signed in multiple identical copies and accepting electronic delivery of signatures. This facilitates execution when parties are not in the same location.
plain_english: This agreement can be signed in multiple identical copies, each considered an original, and sending signed copies by fax or PDF email is as valid as signing and delivering them in person.
